normalized_title: State Planning Policy 2.8 - Bushland Policy for the Perth Metropolitan Region
scope_summary: Provides the policy and implementation framework for protecting and managing regionally significant bushland (Bush Forever areas, established through MRS Amendment 1082/33) and local bushland within the Perth Metropolitan Region. It sets impact assessment processes and criteria for proposals affecting Bush Forever areas, retention benchmarks for negotiated planning solutions, best-practice criteria for rural-living subdivision on Bush Forever rural lands, and offset criteria. It applies to planning proposals and decision-making by the WAPC and local governments, supplementing SPP 2 Environment and Natural Resources Policy.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: each vegetation complex within the Perth Metropolitan Region portion of the Swan Coastal Plain (Bush Forever target)
urban/industrial zoned land in Bush Forever areas developed via stand-alone negotiated planning solutions
rural-living subdivision for conservation in Bush Forever areas (rural lands)
conservation areas set aside over and above the normal 10% public open space contribution and excluded from the developable area used to calculate it
large bush blocks created through rural-living subdivision for conservation

definitions: Government lands or public infrastructure (site implementation category)
Management plans (bushland management plans / environmental management plans)

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 2: Environment and Natural Resources Policy
State Planning Policy 2.4: Basic Raw Materials
Environmental Protection (Clearing of Native Vegetation) Regulations 2004
Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation Act 1999

quality_flags: extracted from the gazette version: gazette page headers ('22 June 2010 GOVERNMENT GAZETTE, WA ...') are interleaved through the text
summary.json title is 'PLANNING AND DEVELOPMENT ACT 2005' (enabling-act header) rather than the policy title
Map 1 (Bush Forever areas) is a drawing - little extractable text for the map, as expected
